topic: CMB Axis of Evil — verdict reclassification (Misleading -> Self-Contradicted) per EXP-295
change_summary: Analyst (EXP-295) recommends Misleading -> Self-Contradicted under sub_rationale 'category-error import (MOND grouping)'. This curmudgeon review verifies the reclassification specifically for WIN-048 (CMB Axis of Evil).
our_argument_summary: WIN-048 currently sits at verdict 'Misleading' with finding 'False dilemma (LambdaCDM problem != dome evidence); anomaly significance eroded by Planck reanalysis; copper firmament (WIN-034) would block CMB microwaves'. The detail_evidence carries four distinct critiques: (a) false-dilemma framing of 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' as dome evidence, (b) Planck reanalysis erosion of statistical significance, (c) self-contradiction with WIN-034's copper firmament (160 GHz CMB cannot pass through copper at any reasonable thickness), and (d) absence of any dome mechanism for CMB generation in flat geometry (no z~1100 photon-baryon fluid oscillations). Critique (c) is explicitly tagged 'Self-contradiction with WIN-034' in the existing prose, and the prose pre-rebuts the metaphorical-defense ('dome model itself uses WIN-034 to provide the conductive ceiling required by WIN-029 Schumann claim — the firmament cannot be metaphorical for one purpose and physical for another'). That language meets SC pattern (2) — structural-coherence failures where the dome's commitments preclude the WIN claim on the dome's own terms — already used for WIN-053 (ring-magnet flux) and WIN-067 (Antarctic gravity rim).

endorsement.rationale: The SC reading is justified by the existing detail_evidence prose. The 'Self-contradiction with WIN-034' inline language is verbatim in the current WIN-048 detail_evidence (data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_evidence). The pre-rebuttal of the metaphorical defense via the WIN-029 Schumann chain is also verbatim. The dome's published cast-copper firmament claim (data/wins.json:WIN-034) is explicit and constitutes a positive physical commitment, even though our review classifies WIN-034 itself as 'Unfalsifiable' (we cannot directly sample the alleged dome material). 'Unfalsifiable on its own merits' does not preclude 'load-bearing as dome commitment for SC pattern (2) reasoning' — see hole #1 on the latent tension. The verdict label is being aligned with what the prose already argues; not introducing a new framework reading.
endorsement.pattern_match: WIN-053 and WIN-067 are the existing SC precedents for pattern (2). WIN-048 fits via the dome's own cast-copper firmament (commitment) precluding 160 GHz CMB throughput (WIN claim of CMB observability as confirming evidence). Stronger than WIN-047 in one sense: the WIN-048 prose articulates a closed dilemma (transparent-firmament defender contradicts WIN-034; thermal-source defender produces featureless blackbody with no acoustic peaks, contradicting CMB observation). Both branches close, which is what SC pattern (2) requires.

relevant_files: raw-text/monitor.py (not re-examined this cycle — last validated 2026-04-07 cycle 2)
dome_code_analysis.note: WIN-048 code_analysis tags were validated in c2/c3; this cycle is a verdict-label change, not a code-tag change. Tags below carry forward from prior cycles unchanged.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: Carried forward — no monitor.py domain tracks the CMB Axis of Evil; the only monitoring artifact is a static live-power dashboard entry (Predicted: >2σ, Observed: 2.5σ, Tolerance: N/A) — a post-hoc threshold against a known result, not a fetch-and-compare monitor.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: Carried forward — there is no standard-physics 'Axis of Evil' explanation that the dome merely renames; standard cosmology treats the alignment as a low-multipole low-significance anomaly. The dome does not predict a different value; it predicts that the anomaly should exist at all and treats the existence as confirmation. No relabeling here, but also no derivation.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Carried forward — Land & Magueijo 2005 (PRL 95, 071301) and Planck PR3/PR4 maps were public well before the dome monitor commit. detail_verdict_text already documents the post-hoc failure: no monitor entry exists for this value at all, so even the weaker commit-timestamp test is inapplicable.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: Carried forward — no derivation chain from disc_radius / firmament_height / sun_altitude / moon_altitude to any CMB observable. The dome offers no mechanism for CMB generation or propagation in flat geometry (the prose explicitly notes this: 'no analog in dome geometry').

holes_found.description: WIN-034's own current verdict is 'Unfalsifiable' (data/wins.json:WIN-034:verdict). The EXP-295 SC reclassification of WIN-048 hinges on treating WIN-034's cast-copper firmament as a load-bearing physical commitment that the dome must defend. A dome defender will spot the latent tension: 'your own review classifies WIN-034 as Unfalsifiable; if it is not a falsifiable physical claim on its own, why are you using it as a falsifying premise to mark WIN-048 as Self-Contradicted?' The existing WIN-048 prose pre-rebuts the metaphorical-defense by chaining to WIN-029 ('dome uses WIN-034 to provide the conductive ceiling required by WIN-029 Schumann claim'), but this chain is fragile: WIN-029 itself argues *against* any solid conductive ceiling being compatible with the measured Schumann Q-factor (data/wins.json:WIN-029:detail_evidence: 'no solid conductive ceiling is compatible with the measured quality factor'). So our own review chain is: WIN-029 says no solid conductive ceiling works, but WIN-034 (Unfalsifiable) is treated as if the dome must defend solid copper, and WIN-048 SC depends on solid copper being a load-bearing dome commitment. This is internally consistent if we read it as 'the dome makes mutually-incompatible commitments' (which IS the SC argument), but the layered structure could be exploited by a defender to claim we are double-counting WIN-034 as 'Unfalsifiable here, falsifying-premise there.'
holes_found.recommendation: Two options for the decider: (a) leave WIN-034's verdict as 'Unfalsifiable' and add a one-line clarification in the WIN-048 audit-trail prose (or in Part 6.13) that 'Unfalsifiable on direct material-sampling grounds does not exempt a published dome commitment from load-bearing-premise status for SC pattern (2) reasoning'; (b) re-examine WIN-034 separately for SC pattern (2) on its own grounds (the dome cannot publish 'cast copper firmament' as conductive-ceiling premise for WIN-029 while simultaneously requiring CMB transparency for WIN-048 — that is a self-contradiction in the dome's commitments, which is exactly SC pattern (2)). Option (b) is out of scope for EXP-295 but flagged here for a future curmudgeon spot-check or analyst proposal. Option (a) is the cheap fix and unblocks the EXP-295 verdict change.

holes_found.description: The 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' sub_rationale label is the weakest fit for WIN-048 of any of the five reclassified WINs. CMB Axis of Evil is not a canonical MOND-community anomaly the way RAR (WIN-052) and El Gordo (WIN-054) are — it is a LambdaCDM-specific low-multipole anomaly that some modified-gravity advocates cite, but it does not appear on McGaugh's Triton Station list as a MOND-distinctive item. The dome's own framing of WIN-048 as a 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' (verbatim in detail_evidence) is closer to 'LambdaCDM-anomaly grab-bag' than 'MOND grouping.' The SC reclassification of WIN-048 is sound on the WIN-034 self-contradiction grounds (independent of MOND-list provenance), but the 'MOND grouping' framing applied uniformly to all five WINs is a pattern-match the analyst is enforcing across the package even where individual WINs fit the pattern differently. (Same shape of critique as raised in the WIN-047 c3 review for the Hubble-Law case.)
holes_found.recommendation: When the EXP-295-P2-WIN-048-verdict patch lands, the sub_rationale text should foreground the WIN-034 / copper-firmament structural argument as the primary basis for SC reclassification, with the MOND-grouping cross-cutting context as secondary. The proposed sub_rationale already gestures at this ('WIN already invokes "Self-contradiction with WIN-034" inline in its detail prose; verdict label now matches the prose. See Part 6.13.'), but consider rewording to make the WIN-034 anchor the lead clause: e.g., 'Self-contradiction with WIN-034 (cast-copper firmament opaque to 160 GHz CMB) is the primary SC basis; cross-cutting category-error context in Part 6.13.' No patch change required — the existing draft is acceptable — but the label 'category-error import (MOND grouping)' is the weakest part of the package as applied to this specific WIN.

holes_found.description: The proposed new tldr_verdict ('The dome's own copper-firmament commitment (WIN-034) makes CMB observation impossible; the dome cannot simultaneously claim CMB anomalies as confirmation. The detail prose already argues this self-contradiction explicitly; the verdict now matches.') has a problematic second sentence. 'The detail prose already argues this self-contradiction explicitly; the verdict now matches' is editor-process commentary about the reclassification, not reader-facing content. It tells the reader 'we are changing the label to match the prose,' which is a meta-statement appropriate for an audit-trail or changelog entry, not a TLDR aimed at non-experts. A TLDR for a SC verdict should anchor the SC argument and stop; the 'verdict now matches' framing leaks the curmudgeon/decider workflow into the user-visible surface.
holes_found.recommendation: Rewrite the second sentence to land on substance, not process. Suggested: 'The dome's own copper-firmament commitment (WIN-034) makes CMB observation impossible: copper at 160 GHz has a skin depth of ~0.2 micrometers, opaque to the very microwaves WIN-048 cites as confirming evidence. The two cannot both be held.' This keeps the SC verdict's structural anchor visible to TLDR-only readers and removes the editor-talk.

holes_found.description: The new tldr_verdict drops two critiques that the original tldr_verdict carried: the false-dilemma framing of 'LambdaCDM Counter-Prediction' (which is the prose's lead argument, not just a side note) and the Planck-reanalysis erosion of statistical significance. Both remain in the detail_evidence and detail_verdict_text, but TLDR-only readers will lose them. This is a defensible TLDR strategy for an SC verdict (anchor the SC argument, let backup arguments live in the expanded prose), but it concentrates the TLDR's load-bearing weight on a single critique. If the WIN-034 SC argument is contested by a defender (see hole #1 on the WIN-034-Unfalsifiable tension), the TLDR offers no fallback; the false-dilemma critique would still apply even if the SC reading were rejected.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider a hybrid TLDR that anchors the SC argument and gestures at the false-dilemma backup: e.g., 'Even setting aside that LambdaCDM anomalies do not constitute dome evidence (false-dilemma framing), the dome's own copper-firmament commitment (WIN-034) makes 160 GHz CMB observation impossible — the two commitments cannot both be held.' This preserves both lines of attack at the TLDR level. Optional improvement; the current draft is acceptable if shortened to remove the 'verdict now matches' meta-clause per hole #3.

holes_found.description: The EXP-295 patches introduce a brand-new 'sub_rationale' field on five WINs simultaneously. Confirmed by inspection: zero existing WINs in data/wins.json carry a sub_rationale field, and grep across build.js / build-scripts/ / test.js shows no rendering pipeline awareness of the field. The Part 6.13 subsection prose references 'sub_rationale category-error import (MOND grouping)' as if it were a published, reader-visible label, but unless the build pipeline is updated, the sub_rationale will be JSON-only metadata invisible to readers. Risk: a defender clicks through to WIN-048 expecting to see the sub_rationale label promised in Part 6.13, and finds nothing. The analyst's EXP-295 itself flagged this ('decider please verify schema since field may need to be added rather than overwritten').
holes_found.recommendation: Decider should either (a) wire sub_rationale rendering into generate-html.js before committing the verdict patches (so the field appears under the verdict badge or in a sub-rationale slot on each WIN's detail block), or (b) downgrade the Part 6.13 reference to 'see audit-trail in EXP-295' rather than implying readers will see the sub_rationale on each WIN page. Option (a) is the cleaner finish and matches the operator's intent of using sub_rationale as a public-facing distinguisher between numerical-conflict SC and framework-level SC. Either way, the decision must precede the verdict-label patches landing on the live site, otherwise Part 6.13 prose ships pointing to non-existent UI.

holes_found.description: WIN-029's detail_evidence does not actually cite WIN-034 as the dome's source for the conductive-ceiling premise (grep WIN-029:detail_evidence for 'WIN-034' returns 0). The WIN-048 prose's chain ('the dome model itself uses WIN-034 to provide the conductive ceiling required by WIN-029 Schumann claim') is true at the dome's level — the dome does invoke copper-firmament as Schumann's conductive ceiling — but our own WIN-029 review prose argues against any solid ceiling being needed (since the ionosphere is already conductive). So the chain that closes WIN-048's pre-rebuttal of the metaphorical defense relies on the dome's own claim structure, not on our review's WIN-029 endorsement of a copper-ceiling necessity. This is fine logically — we are saying 'the dome cannot have it both ways given the dome's own commitments' — but a careful reader who follows the WIN-029 link to verify the chain will find our WIN-029 prose pointed in a different direction (against solid ceilings entirely).
holes_found.recommendation: If the decider extends the WIN-048 prose during EXP-295 integration, consider adding a one-line clarification: 'The dome publishes WIN-034 (cast copper) and WIN-029 (Schumann needs conductive ceiling) as a coupled pair on its own model.html — our critique here addresses the dome's own coupling, not whether a copper ceiling is required for Schumann (we argue it is not — see WIN-029).' Optional; the current chain holds for adversarial-pre-rebuttal purposes even without this clarification.

stronger_arguments.description: The SC reclassification could be sharpened by bringing the closed-dilemma structure into the new tldr_verdict or finding. The WIN-048 prose already articulates BOTH branches that close: (i) transparent-firmament defender contradicts WIN-034's cast-copper claim; (ii) thermal-source defender produces a featureless blackbody at 2.725 K with none of the observed acoustic peaks, E-mode polarization, or gravitational lensing. Naming both branches in the verdict-tier text makes the SC closure visible at a glance — currently the new tldr_verdict only invokes branch (i). Closed-dilemma structure (both defender branches fail) is what makes pattern (2) SC distinct from a one-sided structural objection.

A separate spot-check on WIN-034 itself for SC pattern (2) reclassification could be proposed in a future EXP. The dome's published copper-firmament commitment is incompatible with its own stated CMB observability (WIN-048), with global radio astronomy generally (which observes the universe at frequencies copper would block), and with the observed Schumann Q-factor (which our WIN-029 prose argues precludes any solid conductive ceiling). That is three independent SC dimensions for WIN-034 itself, which would convert it from 'Unfalsifiable' (current) to 'Self-Contradicted' (proposed). This would also resolve the latent tension flagged in hole #1 (WIN-034-Unfalsifiable vs WIN-048-SC-cites-WIN-034).

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome author has correctly identified that the CMB low-multipole alignments (quadrupole-octopole alignment, the so-called 'Axis of Evil') are a real area of cosmological research with non-trivial significance under some statistical estimators (Land & Magueijo 2005 reported 99.4% confidence; Schwarz et al. 2004 documented the ecliptic correlation). Engagement with the literature is correct (data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_evidence). More importantly, the dome author voluntarily corrected the WIN-048 confidence claim on 2026-03-23, downgrading from '>99.9% confirmed by two missions' to '2-3 sigma — not the >99.9% originally reported by WMAP' after the Planck full-mission reanalysis (data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_extra). That self-correction is more intellectual honesty than most advocacy sites manage and our existing detail_extra explicitly credits it.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: The kernel-of-truth (low-multipole anomalies are a real research area; the dome author engages with Planck reanalysis honestly) does not transfer to dome support because the SC argument is structural, not statistical. Whether the Axis of Evil is 6 sigma or 2 sigma or null, the dome's own cast-copper firmament commitment (data/wins.json:WIN-034) precludes 160 GHz microwave throughput at any thickness, which forecloses the dome's ability to observe the CMB at all (data/wins.json:WIN-048:detail_evidence). The thermal-emission defender branch produces a featureless blackbody spectrum incompatible with the observed acoustic peaks, E-mode polarization, and lensing signal. Both defender branches close on the dome's own commitments. The dome's intellectual honesty about the statistical significance does not alter the structural foreclosure — and indeed the dome's correction (2-3 sigma, classified as CONSISTENT rather than confirmed in the dome's own audit text) actually weakens the false-dilemma critique slightly while leaving the SC critique completely intact.

package_summary_for_decider: ENDORSE the EXP-295 reclassification of WIN-048 from Misleading to Self-Contradicted. The proposed verdict change, finding append, and tldr_verdict rewrite are all sound on the structural argument. Four moderate findings: (1) WIN-034's own current verdict is 'Unfalsifiable' which creates a latent tension when WIN-034 is invoked as a falsifying premise for WIN-048 SC — recommend either a one-line clarification in audit-trail prose or a future separate WIN-034 reclassification; (2) MOND-grouping sub_rationale label is weakest fit for WIN-048 specifically — recommend foregrounding the WIN-034/copper anchor in any audit-trail prose; (3) new tldr_verdict's second sentence ('verdict now matches') is editor-process commentary that should be replaced with substantive content; (4) new tldr_verdict drops false-dilemma and Planck-reanalysis critiques entirely — defensible for an SC TLDR but concentrates load-bearing weight on a single critique. Two minor findings: (5) sub_rationale field is brand-new and not yet wired into the build pipeline — decider must wire rendering OR downgrade Part 6.13 reference before patches land; (6) WIN-029 chain in WIN-048's pre-rebuttal is fragile — optional clarification recommended. Net: the verdict change is sound; finding (5) is the only one that could plausibly block the patch landing (UI broken-reference risk).
deeper_analysis: The SC reclassification of WIN-048 is the strongest of the five EXP-295 reclassifications because the prose articulates a closed dilemma (both defender branches fail), which is the textbook structure of pattern (2) SC. The structural argument operates at a different level from the statistical argument, so the 2-3 sigma Planck reanalysis (which the dome author honestly accepted) does not affect the SC reading. The latent WIN-034 tension is real but does not undermine the verdict change — it points to a separate item (WIN-034 itself) that probably warrants reclassification on its own grounds. From a defender's perspective, the strongest pushback is to argue WIN-034 is metaphorical biblical exegesis (currently classified Unfalsifiable for that reason), but the dome's own use of WIN-034 as Schumann conductive-ceiling premise (load-bearing in the dome's coupled-WIN structure) closes that defense. The MOND-grouping framing is the package's weakest claim as applied to WIN-048; a defender who reads Part 6.13 carefully and then opens WIN-048 will see that the MOND-list-import argument applies more loosely here than to the obviously-galactic-scale items (WIN-052 RAR, WIN-054 El Gordo). But the SC verdict label is supportable on the WIN-034 grounds independently of the MOND framing. Net: ENDORSE with the moderate-finding caveats noted, primarily that the sub_rationale rendering question (hole #5) should be resolved before the patches land on the live site.
